title: Using GeoProfile
text: data loaded, must set model MCMC (Monte Carlo Markov Chain) parameters (full details see geoParams() help file). run let’s set sensible sigma settings (sigma represents standard deviation dispersal distribution km). can parameterise prior sigma one three ways: Defining sigma_mean sigma_var Defining sigma_mean sigma_squared_shape Defining sigma_squared_shape example, using sigma_mean sigma_squared_shape parameterise, exact value sigma_var derived calculated follows: α=sigma_squared_shape \alpha = \: \text{sigma_squared_shape} β=exp(2lnσ‾+2ln|Γ(α)|+2ln|Γ(α−0.5)|) \beta = \exp{{\left(2\ln{\bar{\sigma}} + 2\ln{|\,\Gamma(\alpha)|} + 2\ln{|\,\Gamma(\alpha - 0.5)|}\right)}} ϵ=βΓ(α−0.5)Γ(α) \epsilon = \sqrt{\beta} \:\frac{\Gamma(\alpha - 0.5)}{\Gamma(\alpha)} sigma_var=β(α−1)−ϵ2 \text{sigma_var} = \frac{\beta}{(\alpha - 1) - \epsilon^2} MCMC ’ll use 5 chains, discarding 1,000 iterations burnin sampling 10,000 iterations generate final posterior distribution. let’s just check see full parameters object looks like:
code: # set model and MCMC parameters p = gp.params(   data = d,   sigma_mean = 1,   sigma_squared_shape = 2,   chains = 5,   burnin = 1e3,    samples = 1e4   ) #> ℹ Using sigma_mean and sigma_squared_shape to define prior on sigma p #> Geoprofile Parameters (to 4 dp): #>  #> === Model === #> Sigma: mean = 1, var = 0.2732, squared_shape = 2, squared_rate = 1.2732 #> Prior: Mean longitude = -0.1038°, Mean latitude = 51.5175° #> Tau: 6.5177 #> Alpha: shape = 0.1, rate = 0.1 #>  #> === MCMC === #> Burnin: 5 chains @ 1000 iterations (print every 100) #> Sampling: 10000 iterations (print every 1000) #>  #> === Output === #> Longitude range: -0.2061°:-0.0014° #> Latitude range:  51.4915°:51.5435° #> Cells: 500x500

title: Using GeoProfile
text: Now ready actually fit geoprofile model using MCMC approach. Posterior draws smoothed produce posterior surface, converted geoProfile. outputs function include posterior draws alpha sigma variable-sigma model. Note: can control bandwidth posterior smoothing specifying lambda argument. specified, optimal bandwidth chosen via maximum-likelihood. Let’s plot prior posterior sigma. Remember sigma standard deviation dispersal distribution, larger posterior value varied dispersal data.
code: m = geoMCMC(data = d, params = p) #> ✔ Data object passed all checks! #> ✔ Params object passed all checks! #> ── MCMC Log ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── #> Initiating burn-in phase (5 chains) #>   iteration: 100 #>     convergence at the GR=1.1 level reached within 100 iterations #>   iteration: 200 #>   iteration: 300 #>   iteration: 400 #>   iteration: 500 #>   iteration: 600 #>   iteration: 700 #>   iteration: 800 #>   iteration: 900 #>   iteration: 1000 #>     final GR statistic: GR=1.00324 #>  #> Initiating sampling phase #>   iteration: 1000 #>   iteration: 2000 #>   iteration: 3000 #>   iteration: 4000 #>   iteration: 5000 #>   iteration: 6000 #>   iteration: 7000 #>   iteration: 8000 #>   iteration: 9000 #>   iteration: 10000 #>  #> MCMC completed in 0.513 seconds #> ── MCMC Log End ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── #> ℹ Smoothing posterior surface... #> ✔ Maximum-Likelihood lambda = 0.069 geoPlotSigma(params = p, mcmc = m)

title: Using GeoProfile
text: Using wonderful leaflet package, can plot posterior surfaces map! red dots crime reports, blue dots potential sources, heatmap posterior probability surface. can also retrieve hitscores potential sources (measures likely potential source actual source observed events). Lorenz plot data.  can also plot posterior allocation co-allocation   3D plots almost entirely unhelpful data-communication standpoint (unless interactive animated), however can look nice decorative roles. can create using geoPersp()function.   better way visualising data 3D use interactive plot using geoSurface3D() function. gets around problem data occlusion, though still mostly useful decorative scenario. Finding sources often key priority - example malaria may provide efficient method carrying targeted larval source management7. RgeoProfile package uses Dirichlet Process Mixture (DPM) modelling framework Verity et al.7, designed place geographic profiling Bayesian framework deal issue multiple sources. simple terms, DPM model assumes large (strictly infinite) number potential source locations, although sources assumed “active” others. RgeoProfile algorithm attempts infer number active sources (.e. number sources gave rise observed data), along spatial locations sources. using Markov chain Monte Carlo (MCMC) - statistical technique provides estimates unknown parameters iteratively updating guess. They are included here for # completeness) d <- geoData(d$longitude, d$latitude) s <- geoDataSource(s$longitude, s$latitude)  # set model and MCMC parameters p = geoParams(data = d, sigma_mean = 1, sigma_squared_shape = 2, chains = 5,                 burnin = 1e3, samples = 1e4)  #------------------------------------------------------------------ # run model #------------------------------------------------------------------ # run MCMC m = geoMCMC(data = d, params = p)  #------------------------------------------------------------------ # output #------------------------------------------------------------------ # plot prior and posterior of sigma geoPlotSigma(params = p, mcmc = m)  # plot profile on map mapGP <- geoPlotLeaflet(params = p, data = d, source = s, surface = m$geoProfile) mapGP  # get hitscores hs <- geoReportHitscores(params = p, source = s, surface =m$geoProfile) hs  # produce Lorenz plot Gini <- geoPlotLorenz(hit_scores = hs, crimeNumbers = NULL) Gini  # zoom zoomLon = c(-0.1, -0.01) zoomLat = c(51.51, 51.54) mapZoom <- geoPlotLeaflet(lonLimits = zoomLon, latLimits = zoomLat, params = p,                 data = d, source = s, surface = m$geoProfile) mapZoom  # plot allocation geoPlotAllocation(mcmc = m)  # plot co-allocation geoPlotCoallocation(mcmc = m)  # produce perspective plots # probabilities geoPersp(surface = m$posteriorSurface, aggregate_size = 3, surface_type = "prob") # ranked surface geoPersp(surface = m$geoProfile, aggregate_size = 3)  # find centroids of data split by best grouping (placeholder for more thorough method) ms <- geoModelSources(mcmc = m, data = d) ms # add peaks to map # NB requires ggplot2 library(ggplot2) mapSource <- mapGP + geom_point(aes(ms$longitude,ms$latitude), size=6, pch = 3, col="red") mapSource  # plot surface in style of 'unknown pleasures', for fun unknownPleasures(m$geoProfile, paper_ref = "RgeoProfile 2.1.0")  #------------------------------------------------------------------ # compare to alternative ring search strategy #------------------------------------------------------------------ # compare to geoprofile based on ring search strategy surface_ring <- geoRing(params = p, data = d, source = s, mcmc = m) gp_ring <- geoProfile(surface = surface_ring) # map of ring search geoprofile mapRing <- geoPlotLeaflet(params = p, data = d, source = s, surface = gp_ring,                 surfaceCols <- c("red", "white")) mapRing  # hitscores of ring search geoprofile hs_ring <- geoReportHitscores(params = p, source = s, surface = gp_ring) hs_ring  #------------------------------------------------------------------ # incorporate GIS data #------------------------------------------------------------------ # read in north London shapefile as mask and adjust surface north_london_mask <- geoShapefile()  # restrict mask to Tower Hamlets TH_mask <- north_london_mask[which(north_london_mask$NAME == "Tower Hamlets"),] prob_masked <- geoMask(probSurface = m$posteriorSurface, params = p, mask = TH_mask,                 operation = "outside", scaleValue = 1e-9) gp_masked <- geoProfile(prob_masked$prob)  # plot new surface mapMask <- geoPlotLeaflet(params = p, data = d, source = s, surface = gp_masked,                 breakPercent = seq(0,25,l = 11)) mapMask  # hs of masked surface hs_mask <- geoReportHitscores(params = p, source = s, surface = gp_masked) hs_mask  #------------------------------------------------------------------ # } }

text: probSurface original geoprofile, usually object $posteriorSurface produced geoMCMC(). params object produced geoParams(). mask spatial information include. Must one SpatialPolygonsDataFrame, SpatialLinesDataFrame RasterLayer. scaleValue different functions depending value operation. "inside' "outside", value probabilities multiplied inside outside shapefile. "near" "far", scaleValue importance proximity , distance , object described SpatialPolygonsDataFrame, SpatialLinesDataFrame RasterLayer. Thus, default value scaleValue = 1 can increased exaggerate importance proximity distance. used "continuous". operation combine surface new spatial information. Must one "inside", "outside", "near", "far" "continuous". first two multiply areas inside outside area described shapefile (raster) scaleValue. "near" "far" weight geoprofile closeness (distance ) area described shapefile (raster). Finally, "continuous" uses set numerical values (eg altitude) weight geoprofile. NOTE: 'near' 'far' can take minutes run. maths one "add", "subtract", "multiply" "divide". mathematical operation used combine new spatial data geoprofile operation = "continuous".

text: data observations format defined geoData(). sources observations format defined geoDataSource(). sigma_mean mean prior sigma (sigma = standard deviation dispersal distribution) km. sigma_var variance prior sigma km^2. sigma_squared_shape alternative defining prior mean variance sigma, possible directly define parameters inverse-gamma prior sigma^2. , shape parameter inverse-gamma prior. sigma_squared_rate rate parameter inverse-gamma prior sigma^2. priorMean_longitude mean longitude normal prior source locations (degrees). NULL defaults midpoint range data, -0.1277 data provided. priorMean_latitude mean latitude normal prior source locations (degrees). NULL defaults midpoint range data, 51.5074 data provided. tau standard deviation normal prior source locations, .e. far expect sources lie centre. NULL defaults range data plus guard rail either side, c(-0.1377,-0.1177) data provided. latitude_minMax vector containing minimum maximum latitude generate geoprofile. NULL defaults range data plus guard rail either side, c(51.4974, 51.5174) data provided. longitude_cells number cells final geoprofile (longitude direction). Higher values generate smoother distributions, take longer run. latitude_cells number cells final geoprofile (latitude direction). Higher values generate smoother distributions, take longer run. guardRail data input used, longitude_minMax latitude_minMax default range data plus guard rail. parameter defines size guard rail proportion range. example, value 0.05 give extra 5 percent range data.
